title: RefactorExecutor controlado en sandbox
wave: Ola 5 — Sandbox, patch/refactor ejecutable y rollback

architectural_decision.reason: FUNC-SPRINT-43 implementa una capacidad prevista explícitamente por Fase C usando componentes existentes: RefactorPlanner, PolicyEngine, Approval Workflow, ChangeSet, RollbackManager, ReportEngine, EventLogger y SafeSubprocessRunner. No introduce dependencias externas, red, APIs, modelos, Git write, refactor semántico libre, escritura productiva ni deploy.

commands: python -m devpilot_core approval request --tool refactor.sandbox --action execute --subject refactor:RF-001:tests/fixtures/refactor_executor_project --json
python -m devpilot_core refactor sandbox --target tests/fixtures/refactor_executor_project --plan-id RF-001 --approval-id <approval_id> --json
python -m devpilot_core refactor sandbox --target tests/fixtures/refactor_executor_project --plan-id RF-001 --approval-id <approval_id> --run-tests --tests-approval-id <tests_approval_id> --json
pass_criteria: Refactor execution occurs only inside outputs/sandbox.
refactor.sandbox requires scoped approval for side effects.
Unknown or ambiguous plan ids are blocked.
ChangeSet and rollback plan are generated.
block_criteria: Executor accepts unknown, ambiguous or non-deterministic plans.
Arbitrary commands, Git write, LLM calls or external APIs are enabled.
risks: The initial executor supports only mechanical Python text normalization.
Sandbox success can create a false sense of production safety if not reviewed by a human.
Future semantic refactors require AST-aware operations, stronger tests and approval workflows.
Rollback execution remains non-mutating until future restore semantics.
next_sprint: FUNC-SPRINT-44 — Cierre Fase C: repository engineering quality gate
